name: Osso Buco (Gourmet Magazine)
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the veal shanks with salt and pepper. Dredge in flour, shaking off excess.
2. In a heavy skillet, melt 3 tablespoons butter and 3 tablespoons olive oil over medium-high heat. Brown the veal shanks, adding more butter or oil if needed. Transfer browned shanks to a platter. Add wine to the skillet; boil, deglazing the pan until liquid reduces to about half a cup. Reserve in a small bowl.
3. In a flameproof casserole dish large enough to hold veal shanks in one layer, cook onion, carrots, celery, and garlic in remaining 4 tablespoons butter over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally, until vegetables are softened.
4. Add browned shanks and any accumulated juices to the casserole. Pour wine mixture into the pan and add enough broth to almost cover shanks. Scatter tomatoes over shanks; add bouquet garni (6 fresh parsley sprigs, 4 fresh thyme sprigs, 1 bay leaf) and season with salt and pepper.
5. Bring liquid to a simmer over moderately high heat. Braise mixture, covered in the middle of a preheated 325°F oven for 2 hours, or until veal is tender.
6. Transfer veal to an ovenproof serving dish with a slotted spoon; discard strings. Strain pan juices into a saucepan, pressing hard on solids. Skim off fat. Boil for about 15 minutes or until reduced to about three cups.
7. Baste shanks several times with the reduced juices and bake them, basting for an additional 10 minutes, until glazed.
8. In a bowl, combine parsley, lemon zest, and garlic for gremolata. Sprinkle over cooked veal; pour some juice around and over meat. Serve remaining juices in a ladle alongside.
